file_name: Columbia Laboratories, (Bermuda) Ltd. - AMEND NO. 2 TO MANUFACTURING AND SUPPLY AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 Term. Unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 12.2 below, the initial term of this Agreement shall expire on 31 December 2024 (the "Initial Term") unless the Parties mutually agree in writing any extension to the Initial Term. Upon termination of this Agreement, Fleet agrees to perform its obligations under this Agreement until the earlier of [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 Term. Unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 12.2 below, the initial term of this Agreement shall expire on 31 December 2024 (the "Initial Term") unless the Parties mutually agree in writing any extension to the Initial Term. Upon termination of this Agreement, Fleet agrees to perform its obligations under this Agreement until the earlier of six months from the date of termination .
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction in the duration of Fleet's obligations post-termination. Previously, Fleet's obligation continued until a time determined in a confidential way [***]. Now, it states Fleet must continue its obligations for a fixed period of 'six months from the date of termination.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 Term. Unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 12.2 below, the initial term of this Agreement shall expire on 31 December 2024 (the "Initial Term") unless the Parties mutually agree in writing any extension to the Initial Term. Upon termination of this Agreement, Fleet agrees to perform its obligations under this Agreement until the earlier of six months from the date of termination .
perturbation.justification: The original text left open the possibility for the duration of Fleet's obligations post-termination to be determined confidentially. The changed text introduces a fixed period of 'six months from the date of termination,' which contradicts the prior, less defined commitment. This inconsistency in the duration of post-termination obligations creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, thus leading to a strong contradiction.
